'''James Temuera Te Manawanui Bell''' (born 2 May 1994) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|sr}}er for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[NRL]], the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]], and Leigh and [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Bell was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, and is of Scottish<ref>{{cite web|date=8 August 2017|title=Scotland add Two NRL Talents as Walker is withdrawn|url=http://www.rlwc2017.com/news/scotland-add-two-nrl-talents-walker-withdrawn|publisher=RLWC2017.com|access-date=16 August 2017}}</ref> and [[Māori people|Māori]] descent.

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Papakura Sea Eagles]], before being signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2014, Bell played for the New Zealand Warriors' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/b/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140413210942/http://nycdatabase.org/players/b/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=13 April 2014|title=B|date=27 December 2012|access-date=30 July 2017}}</ref> In October 2014, he played in the Warriors' 2014 NYC Grand Final win over the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/leagueunlimited-nyc-teams-2014-grand-final|title=LeagueUnlimited NYC Teams - 2014 Grand Final » League Unlimited|website=League Unlimited|access-date=30 July 2017}}</ref> In 2015, he graduated to the Warriors' [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW|New South Wales Cup]] team and in 2016 he was named the side's man of the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/03/03/vb_nsw_cup_teams_rou.html|title=VB NSW CUP TEAMS ROUND 1|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|date=3 March 2015 |access-date=30 July 2017}}</ref><ref name=awards>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2016/09/13/mannering_s_fifth_pl.html|title=Mannering's fifth player of the year|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|date=13 September 2016 }}</ref> Also in 2016, he captained the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://aucklandleague.co.nz/newsarticle/4749|title=Auckland Rugby League - Auckland Rugby League|website=aucklandleague.co.nz|access-date=30 July 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170730151526/http://aucklandleague.co.nz/newsarticle/4749|archive-date=30 July 2017|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2017===
Bell played in the [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines]].

After making over 50 appearances with the Warriors' reserve team, Bell made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Warriors against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in round 21 of the [[2017 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-warriors-v-sharks/tabid/10874/newsid/110435/default.aspx|title=Updated team lists: Warriors v Sharks|date=28 July 2017 |access-date=30 July 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2017/07/28/_late_mail_bell_call.af_news.html|title=[Late mail] Bell called up for debut|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|access-date=30 July 2017|archive-date=28 July 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170728155109/http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2017/07/28/_late_mail_bell_call.af_news.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 5 November 2020 it was announced that Bell would join [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=5 Nov 2020|access-date=25 Dec 2020|title=James Bell joins Leigh Centurions|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57317/james-bell-joins-leigh-centurions}}</ref>

===St Helens R.F.C.===
On 14 September 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[St Helens R.F.C.]] in the [[Super League]]. Bell played 17 games for St Helens in the 2022 Super League season and featured in the clubs semi-final victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] but he was not included in the grand final team which won their fourth successive title.<ref>{{cite web|website=Manchester Evening News|date=14 Sep 2021|accessdate=22 Sep 2021|title=St Helens complete signing of Leigh Centurions and Scotland star|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-james-bell-leigh-21570031}}</ref>
On 18 February 2023, Bell played in St Helens 13-12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].<ref>{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
Bell played 27 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. He played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which stopped them reaching a fifth successive grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>
In round 11 of the 2024 Super League season, Bell scored two tries for St Helens in their 60-6 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cw590561l69o|title=St Helens romp to 10-try victory at Castleford|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Bell played 24 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. Bell played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
On 16 September 2025, it was announced that Bell would be departing St Helens at the end of the 2025 Super League season. On the same day, it was revealed Bell had signed a contract to join [[Hull F.C.]] starting in 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp8w5xk98nwo/|title=Hull FC sign Batchelor and Bell from St Helens|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
In October, Bell was named in the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2017/10/12/_rlwc2017_bell_the_b.html|title=[RLWC2017] Bell the Braveheart|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|date=12 October 2017 |access-date=16 October 2017}}</ref>
Bell played all three matches for Scotland at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] where they finished bottom of their group.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63438998|title=Rugby League World Cup: Fiji 30-14 Scotland - Bravehearts out in group phase|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
